title: MachinePartInput Object
description: Object representing the set of inputs required to create a new MachinePart. Set an MachineAxisInput object on this object's axisInput parameter to create a new MachineAxis with this part.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: createAxisInput
methods.description: Creates a new MachineAxisInput object to be used to create a new MachineAxis. Set this object on to an axis type MachinePartInput to create a new MachineAxis with that part.

methods.name: createSpindleInput
methods.description: Creates a new MachineSpindleInput object to be used to create a new MachineSpindle.

methods.name: createToolStationInput
methods.description: Creates a new MachineToolStationInput object to be used to create a new MachineToolStation.

properties.name: axisInput
properties.description: Gets or sets an axis input object to create a new MachineAxis with this part. Only valid when partType is Axis.

properties.name: id
properties.description: Gets or sets the internal ID of the part, used to reference this part for other operations. This ID can be any string. This must be unique with respect to other MachineParts in the Machine.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: partType
properties.description: Get the type of part this input will create.

properties.name: spindleInput
properties.description: Gets or sets an spindle input object to create a new MachineSpindle with this part. Only valid when partType is not Axis.

properties.name: toolStationInput
properties.description: Gets or sets an tool station input object to create a new MachineToolStation with this part. Only valid when partType is not Axis.

axisInput.title: MachinePartInput.axisInput Property
axisInput.description: Gets or sets an axis input object to create a new MachineAxis with this part. Only valid when partType is Axis.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = machinePartInput_var.axisInput

# Set the value of the property.
machinePartInput_var.axisInput = propertyValue

id.title: MachinePartInput.id Property
id.description: Gets or sets the internal ID of the part, used to reference this part for other operations. This ID can be any string. This must be unique with respect to other MachineParts in the Machine.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = machinePartInput_var.id

# Set the value of the property.
machinePartInput_var.id = propertyValue

propertyValue = machinePartInput_var.isValid

It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

classType.title: MachinePartInput.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

createAxisInput.title: MachinePartInput.createAxisInput Method
createAxisInput.description: Creates a new MachineAxisInput object to be used to create a new MachineAxis. Set this object on to an axis type MachinePartInput to create a new MachineAxis with that part.
createAxisInput.return_description: Returns a LinearMachineAxisInput or RotaryMachineAxisInput, or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: axisType
parameters.description: The type of MachineAxisInput to create.

createSpindleInput.title: MachinePartInput.createSpindleInput Method
createSpindleInput.description: Creates a new MachineSpindleInput object to be used to create a new MachineSpindle.

createToolStationInput.title: MachinePartInput.createToolStationInput Method
createToolStationInput.description: Creates a new MachineToolStationInput object to be used to create a new MachineToolStation.
